Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Text Ruling

Defendant And Crosscomplainant D 27, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File An Amendment To D 27, Llc'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Sophia Lau, Esq. (323.301.4660; slau@earlysullivan.com) of Early Sullivan Wright Gizer & mcRae LLP for Plaintiffs.
John Hosack Esq. (213.891.0700; jhosack@buchalter.com) of Buchalter for Defendant/Cross-complainant.

Not contested. Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT/Cross-Complainant D 27 LLC'S Motion For Leave To File An Amendment To D 27, LLC'S First Amended Cross-Complaint.

D 27, LLC's motion for leave to amend its first amended cross-complaint to add another cause of action is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP SEC 428.50(c).) Old Republic's chief claim of prejudice is increased litigation costs (Opp. 7:10-11), but such costs invariably flow from an amendment and this amendment changes no core facts.

Old Republic also argues that the amendment will be futile (Opp. 8:18-19), but a proposed amendment's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS OLD REPUBLIC NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY, AND OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Old Republic Title Company's (Old Republic Title) demurrer to the 1st, 2nd, and 6th causes of action in D 27's first amended cross-complaint (FACC) is overruled in its entirety. Old Republic Title's request for judicial notice is denied.

Old Republic Title Company'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of D 27 Llc'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. EDWARD E. SMITH, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for plaintiff and cross-defendant Old Republic National Title Insurance Company and cross-defendant Old Republic Title Company;
Atty. JASON E. GOLDSTEIN, Esq. of Buchalter APC (Tel: 213-891-0700) for defendant and cross-complainant D 27, LLC.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS OLD REPUBLIC NATIONAL TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY, AND OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's Motion To Strike Portions Of D 27 LLC's First Amended Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Old Republic Title's alternative motion to strike punitive damages from the FACC is denied. Punitive damages may be awarded against an escrow agent for violating escrow instructions or concealing information from a party to the escrow, which are among the allegations D 27 brings against Old Republic Title here. (Virtanen v. O'Connell (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 688, 714-715; Moe v. Transamerica Title Ins. Co. (1971) 21 Cal.App.3d 289, 303.)

Old Republic Title's alternative motion to strike attorney fees under the "tort of another doctrine" is denied. "A person who through the tort of another has been required to act in the protection of his interests by bringing or defending an action against a third person is entitled to recover compensation for the reasonably necessary loss of time, attorney's fees, and other expenditures thereby suffered or incurred." (Prentice v. North American Title Guaranty (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618, 620.) More specifically, "[w]hen a paid escrow holder has, as in this case, negligently made it necessary for the vendor of land to file a quiet title action against a third person, attorney's fees incurred by the vendor in prosecuting such action are recoverable as an item of the vendor's damages in an action against the escrow holder." (Id. at 621; see also Tung v. Chicago Title Company (2021) 63 Cal.App.5th 734, at *6.) Here, similarly, D 27 alleges that as a result of Old Republic's negligence, it was required to file suit against the seller to resolve the missing parcel claim, and incurred attorney's fees and costs in so doing. (FACC par. 14.) Old Republic Title concedes that D 27's prayer for attorney's fees in that prior action is proper. (Mot. at 17:22-26.) Further, defendant Old Republic National Title Insurance Company (ORNTIC) is a third party for purposes of this rule. In any event, Old Republic Title's request that the Court strike a portion of that prayer to the extent that it seeks recovery of fees incurred in this action as against it is not a proper subject for a motion to strike.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)
